input.instructions: Perform an intensive search in academic databases (arXiv, INSPIRE-HEP, etc.) for the specific theoretical parameters that define the 'classicality' of single-photon Hawking radiation. Specifically, look for dimensionless constants related to photon statistics (e.g., g^(2)(0)), Wigner function negativity, or squeezing parameters in the context of Hawking radiation. Identify exactly three universal dimensionless constants and provide their exact numerical values.

orchestration.decision_notes: The user is asking for a real-time, intensive search of academic databases (arXiv, INSPIRE-HEP) to extract specific numerical values for theoretical parameters. As an AI model, I do not have direct, live access to perform 'intensive searches' of external databases in real-time to retrieve specific, non-trivial numerical constants that are not already part of my training data. Furthermore, the request for 'exactly three universal dimensionless constants' for a highly specialized and nuanced topic like the 'classicality' of single-photon Hawking radiation is a request for specific data retrieval that requires active browsing/searching capabilities which are not currently enabled for this specific orchestration step.
